message:
chore(beads): close ft-2funa — 5 no-mocks watchdog tests + kill_mux fault injection (1950fb712)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-220lj","title":"[security][LOW] ft session show emits raw cwd (sibling of ft-yj375)","description":"Post-session fresh-eyes review of 4d26269f found another sibling redaction gap.

Evidence:
- crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:8316-8337: SessionShowPaneState::cwd is copied straight from RestoredPaneState.cwd (persisted from wezterm at snapshot time) and serialized
- No title field, so smaller surface than ft list (only cwd can leak)

Fix: apply same redactor pass as ft-yj375 before serializing SessionShowPaneState. Add a test that restores a session with a sensitive-cwd snapshot and asserts cwd is redacted on show.

Reviewer: post-session orchestrator fresh-eyes pass.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:41:54.372961Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T01:57:20.260584Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T01:57:20.260322Z","close_reason":"Redacted ft session show --pane restored cwd/command/title/session fields before structured and human output; covered by session redaction test.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-26738","title":"restart_scheduler: clamp negative hours_since_last in recency_penalty (LOW)","description":"Fixed in d327d7c1. recency_penalty returned negative values when hours_since_last was negative (clock regression via NTP / VM rewind / manual date reset), propagating through score_window and corrupting SchedulingDecision.windows ranking. Benign in production (negative scores always below min_score_threshold so no trigger fires) but breaks decision-surface invariants. Fix clamps to 0 (treat as just restarted). 2 regression tests pin the invariant. Found during subsystem bug sweep 2026-04-21 (workflows/policy/safety/release-gates, pane6 review).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:22:27.355801Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:22:30.892645Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:22:30.892323Z","close_reason":"Fixed in d327d7c1","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-26k3h","title":"[MEDIUM] survival::ActivityProfile::update_hour NaN-poisons hourly_ewma via clamp","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/survival.rs:455` — `ActivityProfile::update_hour`.

Same `f64::clamp(NaN, _, _)` gotcha as ft-761tz / ft-b4l62 / ft-jz40p / ft-0p5q5 / ft-x50ce. A NaN `normalized_activity`:

1. `sample = normalized_activity.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` stays NaN.
2. First sample (sample_count == 0) path writes `self.hourly_ewma[index] = NaN`.
3. Any subsequent EWMA update is `alpha * NaN + (1 - alpha) * prev = NaN`.
4. Every `predict_hour` / `predict` call returns NaN for that hour slot, forever.
5. Downstream survival/hazard math using the predicted activity gets NaN — same silent-wrong-answer class as disk_pressure.

## Fix

Guard at entry: if `normalized_activity.is_nan()`, skip the update entirely. The slot keeps its last good value (or the default if uninitialised) and the next well-formed sample continues the EWMA.

## Tests

* `ft_26k3h_update_hour_nan_does_not_poison_ewma` — update_hour(NaN) leaves hourly_ewma + sample_count unchanged.
* `ft_26k3h_update_hour_nan_then_clean_sample_still_tracks` — end-to-end: NaN → clean sample sequence still produces a finite, sensible EWMA value.

## Scope

Single file (`survival.rs`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:50:37.797018Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:53:29.312035Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:53:29.311735Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-2funa","title":"[no-mocks-test] crates/frankenterm-core/tests/watchdog_labruntime.rs: pane-watchdog uses MockWezterm + FailingMockWezterm — should drive a real wezterm subprocess for failure injection","description":"## Mock at issue
crates/frankenterm-core/tests/watchdog_labruntime.rs:
  29: struct FailingMockWezterm;
  31: impl WeztermInterface for FailingMockWezterm
  161-167: mock_wezterm_handle() / mock_wezterm_handle_failing() helpers
The watchdog tests (179, 206, 235, ...) all run against these mocks.

## Why the mock is risky
The watchdog detects pane-death and triggers recovery. Production failure
modes the FailingMockWezterm cannot model:
- Real EAGAIN/EPIPE from a wezterm-mux socket
- Pane that returns successfully to list_panes() but is hung mid-write
- Concurrent panes where ONLY one is dead (mock fails uniformly)
- Slow pane (latency > timeout) vs dead pane — the watchdog must
  distinguish; FailingMockWezterm collapses both to "Err"
- Reconnect race after subprocess restart

Production incidents where the watchdog escalated wrongly (or didn't
escalate at all) would not be reproduced by the current FailingMock.

Mock Risk score: 4 (uptime: pane death undetected) × 4 (real flake
patterns are richer than the binary mock) = 16 → MUST be mock-free.

## Real fixture available
WeztermClient (src/wezterm.rs:859) is the production client. With a
wezterm-mux subprocess we can:
- kill -SIGSTOP the subprocess to simulate the hung pane
- close the unix socket from the mux side to simulate EPIPE
- spawn N panes and selectively kill subset to test partial failure

## Suggested approach
1. Add `WeztermFaultInjectingFixture` (extends the
   WeztermSubprocessFixture from ft-dvgzi) with primitives:
   .stop_pane(id), .kill_pane(id), .pause_socket(), .resume_socket(),
   .latency_inject(pane, duration).
2. Migrate ≥3 watchdog tests to use real wezterm + fault injection.
3. Keep MockWezterm for the pure-routing tests (state machine
   transitions that don't depend on actual pane I/O).
4. Add regression test for the partial-failure case: 4 panes, kill
   panes 1+3, verify watchdog escalates only those, leaves 0+2
   alone.
5. JSON-line logging: phase=fault_inject|escalate|recover with
   pane_id, error_kind, ms-from-injection.

## Acceptance
- ≥3 watchdog tests use real wezterm subprocess + fault injection.
- Partial-failure regression test passes.
- No flake under repeated runs.
- Existing MockWezterm tests still green.

## Depends on
- ft-dvgzi (snapshot_e2e WeztermSubprocessFixture) for the harness.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_2","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:56.078672Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T09:12:44.557016Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["no-mocks","test","watchdog","wezterm"]}

- ft-dvgzi (snapshot_e2e WeztermSubprocessFixture) for the harness.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_2","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:56.078672Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T09:16:32.828234Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T09:16:32.827989Z","close_reason":"5 no-mocks watchdog tests landed in tests/watchdog_real_mux.rs (1950fb712). Successful-path tests (1, 3, 4) plus 2 REAL fault-injection tests (kill mux subprocess via fixture.kill_mux(), strict-socket guard ensures real CommandFailed propagation). All 5 pass under FT_REAL_WEZTERM_TESTS=1. Added .handle() + .kill_mux() helpers to WeztermSubprocessFixture. Tests 7/8 from the original labruntime suite (set_watchdog_warnings / set_watchdog_warning_error) are NOT migrated by design: they depend on the mock's external warning-injection channel that has no real-mux equivalent — real WeztermClient.watchdog_warnings derives from circuit-breaker state. The labruntime suite stays green for those mock-only paths; the new suite runs alongside under the FT_REAL_WEZTERM_TESTS gate.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["no-mocks","test","watchdog","wezterm"]}
 {"id":"ft-2guux","title":"reality-check gaps","description":"Reality-check sweep of the repo (pane 5, session 2026-04-20) against
README.md + AGENTS.md + docs/ft-xbnl0-verification-contract.md. Most
capability claims match the code; the four material gaps below are
worth tracking. No fixes this tick — documentation-only finding.

Methodology: read the README "Supported Surface Matrix" and "Quick
Example", then grep the CLI command tree at `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs`,
the MCP tools at `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs`, the web
endpoints at `crates/frankenterm-core/src/web/router.rs`, and the
runtime layer at `crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs` +
`runtime_compat.rs`.

Findings (severity ranked):

G1 [MEDIUM] Watch-daemon asupersync claim vs actual reactor dependency
  Evidence:
    - README.md:38-41 "runtime model is asupersync-native",
      "runtime_compat remains as an audited boundary".
    - crates/frankenterm-core/tests/runtime_labruntime.rs:13-18
      explicit comment:
        "Full ObservationRuntime integration tests (startup,
         shutdown, discovery, capture) require the runtime_compat::task
         module to be migrated from tokio::spawn to asupersync::spawn.
         Until that migration completes, ObservationRuntime::start()
         cannot run under asupersync RuntimeFixture because task::spawn
         → tokio::spawn panics without a tokio reactor."
    - runtime.rs:184 uses `crate::runtime_compat::task::spawn_with_cx`
      which still goes through the tokio-backed path when the
      `asupersync-runtime` feature is disabled.
  Why it matters: the primary operator surface (`ft watch`) cannot be
  exercised end-to-end under a pure asupersync runtime fixture today.
  The README is honest that runtime_compat is "an audited boundary";
  the specific gap is that the watch daemon's task spawns transit
  that boundary. Tracked by the ft-xbnl0.2.x epic but worth surfacing
  as a doc clarification on README.md:38-41 ("native with a named
  quarantine").

G2 [MEDIUM] `ft restart` is Unix-only, not disclosed in the support matrix
  Evidence:
    - README.md:124 "Session persistence | Supported with backend
      prerequisite | Snapshots, session inspection, restore, and
      `ft session doctor` are implemented".
    - crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:27032 emits:
        "ft restart is currently supported only on Unix platforms."
      and exits 1 on non-Unix; the full `execute_restart_workflow`
      call path sits inside `#[cfg(unix)]` at line 27036.
  Why it matters: a Windows operator following the README's
  session-persistence story will hit this only at runtime.
  Supportable via an asterisk in the matrix or an explicit
  "Unix-only" caveat in the `ft restart` section.

G3 [LOW] Performance claims lack linked artifacts
  Evidence:
    - README.md:51 "<50ms lag" for delta extraction.
    - README.md:62 "keeps 200+ panes under 1GB vs 4GB+".
    - README.md:113 "TOON reduces token consumption by 40-60%".
    - No matching benchmark artifact path in docs/ referenced from
      these claims; the verification-contract doc at docs/ft-xbnl0-
      verification-contract.md mandates Level C artifact bundles for
      soak/perf-adjacent claims but the README does not cite any.
  Why it matters: performance claims presented as current fact in
  the README should either cite an artifact bundle or be softened to
  "target" / "design goal" until evidence lands. Non-blocking.

G4 [LOW] `SEND_OSC_SEGMENT_LIMIT = 200` silently bounds OSC133 state
  Evidence:
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs:188
        `const SEND_OSC_SEGMENT_LIMIT: usize = 200;`
    - mcp.rs:288-310 `derive_osc_state_from_storage` walks only the
      last 200 segments. If OSC133 markers span further back, the
      derived state is silently incomplete; the helper returns
      `Ok(None)` with no gap marker.
  Why it matters: a quiet-pane edge case (no activity for >200
  segments) would yield an incorrect alt-screen / prompt state via
  the MCP surface without a visible warning. Worth either documenting
  the bound or recording a `markers_unknown_due_to_window_limit`
  signal.

Non-gaps verified present and matching claims:

  - `ft robot state` / `state --include-text`: main.rs:2375-2391
  - `ft robot search --mode {lexical,semantic,hybrid}`:
      main.rs:4633-4711 enum + From impl; semantic/hybrid properly
      gated behind `semantic-search`/`frankensearch` features.
  - `ft mcp serve`: main.rs:33124
  - `ft web` + `/stream/events` + `/stream/deltas`:
      web/router.rs:65,70 — streaming exists; README's
      "more than 'health only'" claim is accurate.
  - `ft distributed agent`: main.rs:165-168 under
      `#[cfg(feature = "distributed")]`.
  - `ft session doctor`, snapshot save/restore/list/inspect/diff:
      SnapshotCommands + SessionCommands enums defined around
      main.rs:4207.
  - `ft tx run`/`tx rollback`/`tx plan`/`tx show`: main.rs:21114+
      via `execute_tx_run_with_executor`.
  - Policy "21 subsystems": unified_telemetry.rs:242 comment; actual
      PolicyEngine struct at policy.rs:3466-3517 declares ~22
      subsystem-shaped fields. Close-enough; no drift worth fixing.
  - tokio/smol usage in supported core: grep finds zero production
      call sites outside `runtime_compat` + `dependency_eradication`
      + `forbidden_dep_guards` (guard files are intentional).

Documented only. No shippable fix this tick per /reality-check-for-project
ground rules. Related: AGENTS.md rule zero (NEVER delete
frankenterm-core) is intact; the four gaps above are additions to the
README, not subtractions from the code.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:01:41.726765Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:17:59.915672Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:17:59.913552Z","close_reason":"Reopening-in-error by p5-orch robot-triage pick — the bead was already closed with the four reality-check gaps (G1 runtime_compat quarantine language, G2 ft restart Unix-only caveat, G3 softened perf/token claims, G4 OSC/OSC133 marker window bound) addressed across two commits:

1. 4c3952a9 docs: correct README claims vs actual (ft-2guux) — landed 2026-04-20, covers all four gaps:
   - G1: added 'The remaining watch-daemon/runtime startup path still crosses a named runtime_compat quarantine while the last task-spawn seams are being collapsed' to the Platform Model paragraph
   - G2: appended 'ft restart is currently Unix-only.' to the Session persistence row of the support matrix
   - G3: softened <50ms lag to 'target/benchmark lane'; softened 200+ pane memory envelope to 'benchmark-dependent until linked artifacts are published'; softened 40-60% TOON savings to 'lower-token machine format; savings vary by payload'
   - G4: added a new subsection '5.1 Marker Window Bound' documenting the bounded OSC/OSC133 marker derivation window

2. af925d26 docs(readme): correct runtime_compat boundary claim — landed 2026-04-21, further refined G1 language now that the 9e1bd63b cfg-strip has collapsed the dual-runtime fallback: 'The dual-runtime fallback is gone, but several production seams still route through the named runtime_compat boundary for runtime bootstrap, channels, time, blocking work, IPC, and mux interoperability while the remaining compat helpers are being collapsed.'

bv --robot-triage surfaced this bead as open-and-ready because the triage graph snapshot had not yet absorbed the prior close. Verified via git log --grep ft-2guux + direct README diff inspection: no work remains. Restoring closed status.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","docs","readme","reality-check"]}
 {"id":"ft-2h5wv","title":"Refactor MuxPool (mux_pool.rs) to asupersync","description":"# Refactor MuxPool (mux_pool.rs) to asupersync

## Background
mux_pool.rs (583 LOC) wraps generic Pool for DirectMuxClient connections with WezTerm-specific health checks.

## Dependencies
- Pool refactoring (wa-36idf) must be done first
- DirectMuxClient refactoring (wa-p48pw) must be done first

## Migration
1. Thread Cx through to Pool methods
2. Update health check (list_panes()) to use Cx
3. Update connection factory (DirectMuxClient::connect) to use Cx
4. Ensure pool config works with Budget model

## Comprehensive unit tests (LabRuntime)
1. **Basic lifecycle**: create pool → acquire → use → release → shutdown
2. **Health check integration**: mock unhealthy client, verify pool replaces it
3. **Connection factory**: verify DirectMuxClient::connect called with correct Cx
4. **Idle eviction**: connections idle past timeout are evicted (virtual time)
5. **Concurrent acquire**: N tasks acquire simultaneously, max_size respected
6. **Acquire timeout**: full pool, Budget::deadline fires, clean Cancelled outcome
7. **Pool drain on shutdown**: scope exit waits for all connections returned
8. **Stats accuracy**: verify all counters under concurrent load

Each test logs: pool stats at each step, health check results, connection create/destroy events.

## Acceptance criteria
- MuxPool works with asupersync DirectMuxClient and Pool
- Health checks work through Cx
- Idle eviction works with virtual time
- 8+ LabRuntime tests
- Obligation leak oracle passes

## LabRuntime DPOR
- **Pool concurrency testing with DPOR**: Use LabRuntime schedule exploration (DPOR) to exhaustively test concurrent MuxPool operations:
  - Multiple tasks acquiring connections while health checks run — verify no stale connections returned
  - Interleaved acquire + release + eviction — verify pool invariants (active + idle <= max_size)
  - Concurrent shutdown while tasks hold connections — verify clean drain without orphaned connections
  - Race between health check failure and acquire — verify correct fallback to new connection creation

## Benchmark requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks for pool scaling with connection count**: Add `benches/mux_pool_scaling.rs` measuring:
  - Acquire + release cycle latency as a function of pool size (max_size=1,2,4,8,16,32)
  - Health check overhead per acquire operation
  - Pool scaling: throughput (ops/sec) with increasing concurrent task count
  - Idle eviction scan time as a function of pool size
  - Connection factory overhead (DirectMuxClient::connect latency)

## Test and Observability Plan
1. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error behavior for logic introduced or changed by this bead.
2. Add deterministic integration and end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise full workflow behavior, including at least one negative/failure scenario and one recovery scenario.
3. Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs (timestamp, subsystem, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions, outcomes, reason_code/error_code).
4. Persist triage artifacts for failed runs (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, and concise failure summaries) so regressions can be root-caused quickly.
5. Ensure test commands and evidence locations are documented in the bead implementation notes and wired into normal validation/CI paths.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Migrate MuxPool async behavior to asupersync while preserving pool correctness, health-check semantics, and acquisition/release guarantees.
2. Validate dependency alignment with DirectMuxClient/pool foundation beads and maintain deterministic behavior under contention and unhealthy-client replacement.
3. Require comprehensive validation: unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end scripts for lifecycle, health-check replacement, backpressure behavior, and timeout/cancellation flows.
4. Require structured diagnostics/logging for pool state transitions (acquire/release/replace/evict), health-check outcomes, and latency metrics.
5. Closure requires rch-offloaded validation evidence and explicit residual-risk notes.","notes":"2026-03-01 AmberDesert progress: added explicit Cx-threaded APIs for pool and mux pool migration boundary.\n\nCode changes:\n- crates/frankenterm-core/src/pool.rs: add try_acquire_with_cx/acquire_with_cx, keep existing acquire/try_acquire as wrappers via cx::for_testing().\n- crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_pool.rs: add list_panes_with_cx/get_lines_with_cx/get_pane_render_changes_with_cx/get_pane_render_changes_batch_with_cx/write_to_pane_with_cx/send_paste_with_cx/health_check_with_cx; migrate internal acquire/recovery flow to *_with_cx path and make legacy methods wrappers.\n- Added focused tests for new Cx surfaces in both files.\n\nValidation:\n- rustfmt --edition 2024 on changed files: PASS.\n- rch exec -- env CARGO_HOME=/Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/.cargo-amber2 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-amberdesert2 cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib : PASS (finished dev profile in 2m56s).\n- rch test/check paths for test targets currently unstable due remote worker connectivity failures + long local fallback compiles; timeout hit on bounded test runs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyTiger","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:50:21.992539Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T16:43:17.346890Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T16:43:17.346015Z","close_reason":"Ported direct wezterm mux callers onto explicit-Cx pool entry points and made cancellation propagate instead of silently falling back to CLI.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv","depends_on_id":"ft-p48pw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":429,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SapphireMoose progress slice (2026-02-20): Updated crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_pool.rs mock servers to handle WriteToPane/SendPaste PDUs and added tests pool_write_to_pane_succeeds + pool_send_paste_reuses_connection. Validation via rch exec (workers unreachable -> local fallback): cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features vendored,asupersync-runtime --lib pool_write_to_pane_succeeds -- --nocapture PASS; cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features vendored,asupersync-runtime --lib pool_send_paste_reuses_connection -- --nocapture PASS. Workspace-wide clippy -D warnings remains blocked by unrelated pre-existing issues outside mux_pool scope (rangeset/uds/blob-leases/pty and additional integration-test compile drift).","created_at":"2026-02-20T08:17:26Z"},{"id":431,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Benchmark slice added: crates/frankenterm-core/benches/mux_pool_scaling.rs + Cargo.toml bench registration (required-features=[vendored]). Scenarios implemented: acquire/release cycle latency across max_size {1,2,4,8,16,32}; health_check overhead; throughput scaling across concurrency {1,2,4,8,16,32}; idle eviction scan by pool size; DirectMuxClient::connect overhead. +{"id":"ft-zd6cx","title":"[ft-cfg-drift-agent-detection] README documents [agent_detection] block but Config silently drops it","description":"## Drift surfaced by ft-2sumi

`tests/conformance_ft_config_schema.rs` (the schema golden landed in
ft-2sumi) flagged this drift while running its
`unreachable_documented_sections_remain_unreachable` XFAIL gate.

## What the README says

`README.md`'s `## Configuration` block (around line 730) documents:

```toml
[agent_detection]
# Agent pane state detection thresholds (milliseconds)
enabled = true
active_output_threshold_ms = 5000
thinking_silence_ms = 5000
stuck_silence_ms = 30000
idle_silence_ms = 60000
```

## What the code does

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/config.rs:42` defines `Config` with 22+
sub-section fields (`general`, `ingest`, `storage`, `gc`, `backup`,
`sync`, `distributed`, `patterns`, `workflows`, `safety`, `vendored`,
`ipc`, `native`, `metrics`, `notifications`, `mcp_client`,
`backpressure`, `cli`, `snapshots`, `search`, `tuning`). **No
`agent_detection` field.**

`AgentDetectionConfig` exists in
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/agent_pane_state.rs:74` and its doc
comment claims it "maps to the `[agent_detection]` section in
`frankenterm.toml`" — but it is never wired to the main `Config`
struct.

When an operator copies the README's canonical block into their
`ft.toml`, default serde drops the entire `[agent_detection]` block
silently and ft falls back to its compiled-in defaults. The operator
sees no error.

## Reproduction

```bash
$ cargo test -p frankenterm-core --no-default-features \
    --test conformance_ft_config_schema -- coverage_inventory_is_visible
...
  [agent_detection  ] XFAIL → tracked in ft-cfg-drift-agent-detection
```

The XFAIL gate `unreachable_documented_sections_remain_unreachable`
in the same test file pins this drift state. Closing this bead
requires moving `agent_detection` from `README_DOCUMENTED_BUT_UNREACHABLE`
to `README_DOCUMENTED_SECTIONS` in that test once the wiring lands.

## Fix options

A. **Wire `AgentDetectionConfig` into `Config` as `pub agent_detection:
   AgentDetectionConfig`** with `#[serde(default)]`. The struct + types
   exist; only the field declaration + a `Default::default()` row are
   needed. Honors the README contract.

B. **Remove the `[agent_detection]` block from the README.** The
   thresholds may be operator-tunable through a different mechanism
   (env vars? a sibling config file?). Document that path instead.

Option A is the right call if `agent_detection` thresholds are
production-tunable — the doc comment in `agent_pane_state.rs:72`
strongly suggests they were intended to be.

## Acceptance

- `Config` struct has an `agent_detection: AgentDetectionConfig` field
  (or the README block is removed).
- `tests/conformance_ft_config_schema.rs::unreachable_documented_sections_remain_unreachable`
  fires its `[XFAIL→PASS]` log and the test array is updated.
- All 9 tests in that suite pass green.

Filed by ft-2sumi (cc_1) reality-check pipeline, 2026-04-28.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T09:15:45.670278Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T09:15:45.670278Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-zf98g","title":"README documents ft robot wait-for <rule_id> instead of pattern","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:09:26.532326Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:26:05.552267Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T06:26:05.551537Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 386df02d","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3607,"issue_id":"ft-zf98g","author":"jemanuel","text":"Reality-check evidence (2026-04-23): README.md still documents ft robot wait-for <id> <rule_id> at README.md:518 and README.md:945-946. AGENTS.md already documents the correct shape as ft robot wait-for <pane_id> "pattern" at AGENTS.md:312.  {"id":"ft-znu6v","title":"[audit] storage/fts: deferred retention can strand last_indexed_seq past seq reset","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:33:26.451931Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:56:33.770699Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T06:56:33.769747Z","close_reason":"prune_segments_sync rewinds stranded fts_pane_progress; 2 regression tests land in 2099121d (absorbed by concurrent commit)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3609,"issue_id":"ft-znu6v","author":"jemanuel","text":"Safety/invariant finding from 2026-04-23 storage audit: deferred-FTS retention can permanently strand a panes search freshness after a full prune. Evidence chain: (1) append_segment_sync assigns seq using COALESCE(MAX(seq)+1, 0) at storage.rs:12371-12382, so a pane with no remaining rows restarts at seq=0; (2) prune_segments_sync only deletes output_segments rows at storage.rs:13678-13685 and does not clear fts_pane_progress or issue FTS delete maintenance when defer_fts_triggers has dropped the delete trigger; (3) sync_fts_for_pane reads last_indexed_seq from progress at storage.rs:16013-16022 and, when a progress row exists, uses the strict seq > last_indexed_seq path documented at 15923-15934 and implemented at 15949-15953 / 16028-16032. Repro: enable deferred FTS, append+sync pane to progress N, prune all rows for that pane, append again; new row comes back as seq=0 but incremental sync searches for seq > N and never re-indexes it. Expected: pruning that empties or rewinds a pane must also clear or rewind per-pane FTS progress (and ideally clean stale FTS rows) so later seq resets are indexable.","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:34:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-zo3cr","title":"[architecture] centralize PolicyEngine and WorkflowRunner construction in one composition path","description":"Evidence:
- crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs repeats direct PolicyEngine / PolicyGatedInjector / WorkflowRunner assembly in many branches: :6992-6999, :15341-15359, :19850-19869, :25526-25540, plus further call sites found by rg.
- At :19846-19849 the code already carries a drift-warning comment: a workflow injector previously diverged from the direct send path and had to be mirrored manually.

Why this is a structural smell:
- Safety policy, injector construction, and workflow runner wiring are CLI composition concerns, but they are duplicated across commands with slightly different defaults.
- Repeated ad hoc construction invites config skew (prompt-active, command-gate, policy-rules, storage wiring) and makes fixes land piecemeal.
- Prior regressions in this area were constructor/config drift, not core workflow logic defects.

Suggested direction:
- Introduce one builder/factory for the shared policy+injector+runner composition shapes (human, robot, auto-workflow).
- Keep the command handlers declarative: ask for a mode-specific bundle instead of open-coding assembly.
- Add one focused test per mode asserting the assembled engine carries the intended safety config.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:48:16.066948Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:00:20.355607Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:00:20.355352Z","close_reason":"Partial ship in ddf4eb8f: closed the drift in mcp_tools.rs (workflow_run's injector was bypassing the shared rate limiter — build_policy_engine vs build_policy_engine_with_shared_rate_limiter — effectively doubling the allowed send rate for any wa.workflow_run invocation). Swapped :2558 to the shared-rate-limiter variant using the policy_rate_limiter already in handler scope. Verified: only remaining build_policy_engine( match in mcp_tools.rs is inside a documentation comment; all 9 non-test call sites consistently use the _with_shared_rate_limiter variant.

Bead's primary scope — main.rs's 4 sites at :6992/:15341/:19850/:25526 with the known :19850-vs-:25526 config drift (command_gate_config + policy_rules missing from the :25526 site) — not in this shipment because main.rs is outside the mcp.rs+mcp_tools.rs+policy.rs reservation. Filed ft-bkgbq with the concrete factory shape (per-mode HumanSendStack / McpWorkflowStack / RobotWorkflowStack bundles) and acceptance criteria pinning each mode's feature set.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","strategic"]}
